00051| - Orchestrator Session Lifecycle
00052|   - See: /Users/dylanconlin/Documents/personal/orch-go/.kb/models/orchestrator-session-lifecycle.md
00053|   - Summary:
00054|     Orchestrator sessions operate in a **three-tier hierarchy** (meta-orchestrator → orchestrator → worker) where each level is completed by the level above. Orchestrators produce **SESSION_HANDOFF.md** (not SYNTHESIS.md) and **wait** for completion (not /exit). They track via **session registry** (not beads) because orchestrators manage conversations, not work items. Frame collapse occurs when orchestrators drop levels and do work below their station - detected externally, not self-diagnosed. Checkpoint discipline uses duration thresholds (2h/3h/4h) as a proxy for context exhaustion.
00055|     
00056|     ---
00057|   - Why This Fails:
00058|     ### 1. Frame Collapse (Orchestrator → Worker)
00059|     
00060|     **What happens:** Orchestrator drops into worker-level implementation (editing code, debugging, investigating).
00061|     
00062|     **Root cause:** Vague goals → exploration mode → investigation → debugging. **Framing cues override skill instructions.**
00063|     
00064|     **Why detection is hard:** Orchestrators can't self-diagnose frame collapse. The frame defines what's visible, so from inside the collapsed frame, the behavior feels appropriate.
00065|     
00066|     **Detection signals:**
00067|     - Edit tool usage on code files (not orchestration artifacts)
00068|     - Time spent >15 minutes on direct fixes
00069|     - SESSION_HANDOFF.md shows "Manual fixes" sections
00070|     - Post-mortem reveals work that should have been spawned
00071|     
00072|     **NOT the fix:** Adding more ABSOLUTE DELEGATION RULE warnings. The agent already knows. The problem is framing, not awareness.
00073|     
00074|     **Prevention:**
00075|     1. Provide specific goals with action verbs, concrete deliverables, success criteria
00076|     2. Use WHICH vs HOW test: meta decides WHICH focus, orchestrator decides HOW to execute
00077|     3. Frame collapse check in SESSION_HANDOFF.md template
00078|     4. Potential: OpenCode plugin tracking Edit usage on code vs artifacts
00079|     
00080|     **Trigger pattern:** Failure-to-implementation. After agents fail, orchestrator tries to "just fix it" instead of trying different spawn strategy.
00081|     
00082|     ### 2. Self-Termination Attempts
00083|     
00084|     **What happens:** Spawned orchestrator tries to run `orch session end` or `/exit` instead of waiting for completion.
00085|     
00086|     **Root cause:** ORCHESTRATOR_CONTEXT.md template contradicted the hierarchical completion model (told orchestrator to self-terminate).
00087|     
00088|     **Why it's wrong:** Breaks the "completed by level above" invariant. Orchestrator can't verify its own work from meta perspective.
00089|     
00090|     **Fix:** Template updated Jan 2026 to instruct "write SESSION_HANDOFF.md and WAIT".
00091|     
00092|     ### 3. Session Registry Drift
00093|     
00094|     **What happens:** `~/.orch/sessions.json` shows status "active" for completed sessions.
00095|     
00096|     **Root cause:** `orch complete` didn't update session registry status (only closed beads issues or removed registry entries).
00097|     
00098|     **Why it matters:** Stale active sessions accumulate, `orch status` shows ghost sessions, registry becomes unreliable.
00099|     
00100|     **Fix:** `orch complete` now updates status to "completed", `orch abandon` updates to "abandoned" - sessions preserved for history, not removed.

00957| 
00958| **Purpose:** Evaluate feature ethics BEFORE implementation. Distinct from Security Review (code quality) - this is about feature design itself.

00963|

00991|
